title: Dusted Crispy Cornish Beef Pasty
description: A mustardy, crispy one‑pot Cornish pasty made with beef, bacon, cauliflower, and a sweet‑savory mother sauce.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200°C and lightly grease a ceramic tagine with olive oil.
Cook the diced bacon over medium heat until crisp, then set aside on a paper-towel line.
In the same skillet, brown the beef cubes in batches, seasoning each with salt, pepper, garlic powder, then transfer to the tagine.
Add the diced onion to the skillet and sauté until translucent, then stir into the tagine with the beef.
Mix in the cauliflower florets, mustard, brown sugar, and soy sauce, coating evenly and letting the mixture simmer for 10 minutes.
Sprinkle the whole wheat flour over the mixture, stir to thicken the sauce into a mother-sauce base that clings to the ingredients.
Return the cooked bacon to the tagine, toss to combine all flavors, and steam for 15 minutes until the cauliflower is tender.
Sprinkle the dish with additional mustard for a bright finish before baking.
Transfer the tagine to a covered ceramic dish and bake at 200°C for 20 minutes, then uncover and bake for another 10 minutes until the top is crisp and golden.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with fresh herbs, and enjoy the crispy, mustardy pasty.
